key_predictive_features: BTC price action (90-day correlation ~0.85 historically, but ETH/BTC ratio currently at multi-year lows)
DeFi TVL and stablecoin usage growth
Whale exchange withdrawals (120K ETH withdrawn Mar 2026)
Institutional accumulation rate (2x BTC pace per Standard Chartered)
fundamental_drivers: L2 ecosystem growth vs value capture dilution
Staking yield vs real interest rates

cross_asset_correlations.BTC: Primary driver. 90-day rolling correlation near 0.85-0.97. ETH rarely diverges from BTC direction.
cross_asset_correlations.DXY: Inverse correlation. Strong USD weakens ETH. Currently relevant with Iran/oil situation.
cross_asset_correlations.US_10Y: Moderate inverse. Rising yields compress growth/risk-on assets.
cross_asset_correlations.SPX: Moderate positive. ETH trades as risk-on asset.

seasonal_patterns: Q1 typically strong (January effect carries into Feb-Mar)
Q2 historically mixed - post-halving cycles tend to peak later
papers_and_models: On-chain metrics (active addresses, exchange flows) are the most predictive non-price features
BTC-ETH spread models: trade ETH when BTC/ETH ratio is at extremes
Funding rate + Open Interest as short-term predictors
recommended_new_signals: ETH/BTC ratio mean-reversion signal (ratio at multi-year lows = bullish for ETH relative)
DeFi TVL change signal (proxy for ETH network demand)
ETH staking rate change (reduced staking = more liquid supply = bearish)
deep_dives.implementation_notes: Primary issue: our signals only achieve 48.1% on ETH at 3h. The BTC correlation is so high that ETH-specific signals add little value. Consider: (1) Use BTC consensus as a leading indicator for ETH, (2) Only generate ETH signals when ETH/BTC divergence is detected, (3) Add ETH/BTC ratio as cross-asset signal.

key_predictive_features: Gold/Silver ratio (currently compressed from 90 to ~63 based on 4766/75.90)
DXY inverse correlation (strong USD = weak metals)
US real yields (TIPS spread) - negative real yields = bullish metals
Industrial demand metrics (solar installations, EV production)
ETF flow data (iShares Silver Trust SLV)
fundamental_drivers: Industrial demand from solar/EV/AI data centers
Central bank gold buying creating spillover demand

cross_asset_correlations.XAU: Primary driver. Gold leads, silver follows with higher beta. 95%+ directional agreement.
cross_asset_correlations.DXY: Strong inverse. Dollar weakness = metals strength.
cross_asset_correlations.VIX/GVZ: Moderate positive in risk-off. Silver drops initially, then rebounds with gold.

seasonal_patterns: Q1 and Q4 typically strongest (Indian wedding season, solar panel manufacturing ramp)
FOMC meetings can create short-term volatility but net direction depends on outcome
papers_and_models: Gold/Silver ratio mean-reversion: ratio above 80 = buy silver, below 50 = sell. Currently ~63 (neutral)
COT positioning as contrarian indicator: extreme spec longs = caution
Supply deficit model: 6 years of deficits suggests structural floor
Silver/copper ratio as industrial demand proxy
recommended_new_signals: COT net positioning change (already registered but force-HOLD - consider enabling with live validation)
Gold/Silver ratio velocity (rapid compression = bullish silver momentum)
SLV ETF flow signal (large inflows = institutional buying confirmation)
deep_dives.implementation_notes: XAG is the users primary focus. 53.5% at 3h is barely above random. Metals LLM (Ministral) at 40.9% for XAG 3h is harmful. Key improvement: the metals_cross_asset signal already captures copper/GVZ/G-S ratio but may not be weighted enough. Also COT_positioning is force-HOLD - enabling it with proper validation could help. The structural supply deficit story suggests a long-term bull bias that short-term signals struggle to capture.

key_predictive_features: NAV premium/discount (Premium contributes 96.5% of return variance)
BTC acquisition announcements (weekly purchases moving stock price)
Convertible bond issuance (dilution vs BTC accumulation)
Options implied volatility (113% HV vs BTC 55%)
fundamental_drivers: Pure BTC proxy with leverage. 766,970 BTC holdings (~$58B)
NAV premium fluctuates: expands in bull markets, compresses in bear
Debt structure: convertible bonds with BTC-denominated upside
TD Cowen PT $385 from $350. Institutional coverage expanding

cross_asset_correlations.BTC: DOMINANT. 0.97 correlation. MSTR is BTC with 1.77x beta.
cross_asset_correlations.Nasdaq: Moderate positive. Trades as high-beta tech/crypto proxy.
cross_asset_correlations.VIX: Moderate inverse. Risk-off selloffs hit MSTR hard.

seasonal_patterns: No strong seasonality independent of BTC
BTC acquisition announcements (weekly Monday filings) create recurring catalysts
Earnings quarters can create short-term volatility (software biz is secondary)
papers_and_models: NAV premium model: MSTR price = BTC_per_share * premium_multiplier
Premium expands with BTC momentum, compresses with macro fear
GARCH volatility: extreme persistence, leverage effects
recommended_new_signals: BTC consensus as direct MSTR signal (use BTC signal output for MSTR decisions)
NAV premium/discount reversion signal (extreme premium = sell, extreme discount = buy)
Weekly BTC acquisition tracking (larger purchases = bullish catalyst)
deep_dives.implementation_notes: MSTR at 46.5% accuracy is BELOW coin flip. The issue: we run all 32 signals on MSTR as if its a regular stock, but its really a BTC derivative. Most stock signals (fundamental, earnings, etc.) add noise. Solution: use BTC consensus directly as primary MSTR signal, supplement with NAV premium analysis. This would likely boost accuracy above 50%.
